item.title: He Walked 28 Miles to Kill His Ex
item.description: Daniel Boulton’s obsession with his ex-partner turned into one of Britain’s most horrifying domestic violence cases. On May 31, 2021, despite being under a restraining order, he walked 28 miles to Bethany Vincent’s home in Lincolnshire and lay in wait before launching a brutal attack. Bethany was stabbed nine times, while her 9-year-old son, DJ Henson, suffered at least a dozen stab wounds. After fleeing the scene, Boulton sparked a 24-hour manhunt and even stabbed an off-duty police officer before being captured. In 2022, he was sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 40 years.

media:content.media:title: He Walked 28 Miles to Kill His Ex
media:content.media:description: Daniel Boulton’s obsession with his ex-partner turned into one of Britain’s most horrifying domestic violence cases. On May 31, 2021, despite being under a restraining order, he walked 28 miles to Bethany Vincent’s home in Lincolnshire and lay in wait before launching a brutal attack. Bethany was stabbed nine times, while her 9-year-old son, DJ Henson, suffered at least a dozen stab wounds. After fleeing the scene, Boulton sparked a 24-hour manhunt and even stabbed an off-duty police officer before being captured. In 2022, he was sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 40 years.

item.title: Liam Reid's Reign of Terror in New Zealand
item.description: The chilling case of Liam Reid, a figure whose heinous crimes left New Zealand reeling. This episode delves into the details of the attacks, the tragic loss of life, and the manhunt that ended in Reid's capture. We explore the psychological profile of a killer, the impact on the victims' families, and the broader societal implications of such brutal acts. What are your thoughts on this case?

media:content.media:title: Liam Reid's Reign of Terror in New Zealand
media:content.media:description: The chilling case of Liam Reid, a figure whose heinous crimes left New Zealand reeling. This episode delves into the details of the attacks, the tragic loss of life, and the manhunt that ended in Reid's capture. We explore the psychological profile of a killer, the impact on the victims' families, and the broader societal implications of such brutal acts. What are your thoughts on this case?

item.title: The Stepmother Who Thought She'd Get Away With It
item.description: In January 2020, 11-year-old Gannon Stauch vanished from his Colorado Springs home after his stepmother, Letecia Stauch, claimed he had gone to a friend's house and never returned. As search teams and volunteers desperately looked for the missing boy, investigators uncovered a far darker truth. Authorities alleged Letecia attacked Gannon inside his home, inflicting blunt-force, sharp-force, and gunshot injuries before hiding his body in a suitcase. Weeks later, his remains were discovered beneath a bridge in Florida, more than 1,300 miles away. Prosecutors described an elaborate cover-up involving lies, false stories, and attempts to mislead investigators. In 2023, Letecia was convicted of murder and sentenced to life without parole. The heartbreaking case shocked America and raised haunting questions about trust, deception, and the dangers that can exist inside a family home.

media:content.media:title: The Stepmother Who Thought She'd Get Away With It
media:content.media:description: In January 2020, 11-year-old Gannon Stauch vanished from his Colorado Springs home after his stepmother, Letecia Stauch, claimed he had gone to a friend's house and never returned. As search teams and volunteers desperately looked for the missing boy, investigators uncovered a far darker truth. Authorities alleged Letecia attacked Gannon inside his home, inflicting blunt-force, sharp-force, and gunshot injuries before hiding his body in a suitcase. Weeks later, his remains were discovered beneath a bridge in Florida, more than 1,300 miles away. Prosecutors described an elaborate cover-up involving lies, false stories, and attempts to mislead investigators. In 2023, Letecia was convicted of murder and sentenced to life without parole. The heartbreaking case shocked America and raised haunting questions about trust, deception, and the dangers that can exist inside a family home.

item.title: The Nerdy IT Worker Who Secretly Controlled Their Lives
item.description: Stephen and Carol Baxter appeared to be a happy retired couple living in the seaside town of West Mersea, England. But behind the scenes, they had unknowingly become victims of a chilling betrayal. In April 2023, their trusted family friend, 34-year-old IT worker Luke D’Wit, secretly poisoned them with lethal doses of fentanyl after years of manipulation. Using more than 20 fake online identities, he gained their trust, controlled Carol’s health decisions, and isolated the family. After their deaths, D’Wit even rewrote their will to seize control of their business. Investigators later discovered he had monitored the couple through hidden surveillance and watched them die remotely. In 2024, he was convicted of double murder and sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 37 years. What drove a seemingly ordinary man to commit such a calculated and disturbing crime?

media:content.media:title: The Nerdy IT Worker Who Secretly Controlled Their Lives
media:content.media:description: Stephen and Carol Baxter appeared to be a happy retired couple living in the seaside town of West Mersea, England. But behind the scenes, they had unknowingly become victims of a chilling betrayal. In April 2023, their trusted family friend, 34-year-old IT worker Luke D’Wit, secretly poisoned them with lethal doses of fentanyl after years of manipulation. Using more than 20 fake online identities, he gained their trust, controlled Carol’s health decisions, and isolated the family. After their deaths, D’Wit even rewrote their will to seize control of their business. Investigators later discovered he had monitored the couple through hidden surveillance and watched them die remotely. In 2024, he was convicted of double murder and sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 37 years. What drove a seemingly ordinary man to commit such a calculated and disturbing crime?

item.title: Teen's Terrifying End by Her Mum's Boyfriend | Riley Crossman
item.description: This episode retraces the final days leading up to Riley's heartbreaking disappearance, the tireless search by the community, and the devastating discovery of her remains. We examine the incriminating evidence that pointed to McCauley, the emotional toll on Riley's loved ones, and the legal journey that culminated in his arrest and conviction. Through Riley's story, we discuss the importance of safeguarding our communities and the ongoing fight to ensure such tragedies do not recur. Have you come across this case before?

media:content.media:title: Teen's Terrifying End by Her Mum's Boyfriend | Riley Crossman
media:content.media:description: This episode retraces the final days leading up to Riley's heartbreaking disappearance, the tireless search by the community, and the devastating discovery of her remains. We examine the incriminating evidence that pointed to McCauley, the emotional toll on Riley's loved ones, and the legal journey that culminated in his arrest and conviction. Through Riley's story, we discuss the importance of safeguarding our communities and the ongoing fight to ensure such tragedies do not recur. Have you come across this case before?

item.title: A Teacher's TWISTED Mind: Fiona Beal
item.description: When Fiona Beal decided to kill her partner Nicholas Billingham she planned it to the last moment. What are your thoughts on this case?

media:content.media:title: A Teacher's TWISTED Mind: Fiona Beal
media:content.media:description: When Fiona Beal decided to kill her partner Nicholas Billingham she planned it to the last moment. What are your thoughts on this case?

item.title: Teacher Turns Evil: Monica Elizabeth Young
item.description: Monica Elizabeth Young was just 23 when she began grooming a 14-year-old boy via Snapchat, then used her position of trust in a Sydney school to take things further in the classroom. This is another case which shows you that not all predators are male.

media:content.media:title: Teacher Turns Evil: Monica Elizabeth Young
media:content.media:description: Monica Elizabeth Young was just 23 when she began grooming a 14-year-old boy via Snapchat, then used her position of trust in a Sydney school to take things further in the classroom. This is another case which shows you that not all predators are male.
